summary: Good‑quality soundboard recording of a celebratory late‑90 show with standout Stagger Lee, Victim Or The Crime and a soaring Dark Star
review: The UltraMatrix SBD capture is generally clear but suffers from an overly bright Vince mix that can mask details early in the set; it improves after the drums come in. Musically the concert is one of the high points of the 1990 run, highlighted by a superb Stagger Lee, a sterling To Lay Me Down, a powerful Victim Or The Crime and a memorable Dark Star that thrilled first‑time listeners.

ai_rating.rationale: The reviewer praises the performance heavily (standout songs, high overall rating) while noting some mix issues; weighting show quality higher yields a strong but not perfect score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Updated UltraMatrix SBD is patched and pitched; Vince's keyboard was mixed too high FOH early on, making the first set a bit harsh. The drums become listenable later, and overall the sound is clear enough for enjoyment.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set solid but hampered by a bright keyboard mix; second set builds excitement with Victim, Dark Star and Miracle before a sweet Knockin' on Heaven's Door encore, delivering an overall celebratory arc.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Adds minimal but effective guitar on Lovelight; his playing contributes to the electric charge of the night.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince’s keyboard was mixed too high early, yet his playing shines on Dark Star, Man Smart (Woman Smarter) and other numbers.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums become audible and listenable later in the set, improving the overall mix.
